rulings.Case Title: JOHN GIDDING et al VS. TOM CYGNAR et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Tuesday, November 15, 2011, Line 1. DEFENDANTS TOM CYGNAR, CLAUDIA CYGNAR, AND SPRINGSROAD CONSULTING'S DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT. Sustained with ten days leave to amend. A comparison of the allegations in the amended complaint with the allegations in the first amended complaint in 467958 shows that the current allegations either repeat what had been alleged in the prior action or could have been raised in the prior action and the judgment and rulings in the prior action bars assertion of the current allegations against the demurring defendants under res judicata and collateral estoppel. Mr. Gidding is granted leave to file an amended complaint to allege, if he can do so, in good faith, allegations of misconduct against the demurring defendants that were not alleged in the prior action or could not have been raised in the prior action. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. = (302/HEK)

rulings.Case Title: JOHN GIDDING et al VS. TOM CYGNAR et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Tuesday, November 15, 2011, Line 1. DEFENDANTS STEVE VENTRELLO, FAITH VENTRELLO, VINTAGE WINE MARKETING AND VENTRELLO WINE MARKETING'S DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT. Sustained with ten days leave to amend. Plaintiff John Gidding¿s claim is based on alleged misconduct committed against a company for which he was a former shareholder and a guarantor. Because such a claim belongs to the company, Mr. Gidding lacks standing to sue for the sole claim alleged in the amended complaint. Mr. Gidding is granted leave to file an amended complaint to allege, if he can do so in good faith, that the demurring defendants or any of them committed misconduct against him personally and he suffered damage therefrom. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. = (302/HEK)

rulings.Case Title: JOSEPH RATTI VS. CITY AND COUNTY OF SAN FRANCISCO et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Ntc Of Mtn To Strike Deft'S Second Mtn For Summary Judgment/Adjudication And Req. For Judicial Ntc
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Tuesday, November 15, 2011, Line 2. PLAINTIFF JOSEPH RATTI'S Notice Of Motion To Strike Defendant'S Second Motion For Summary Judgment/Adjudication. Granted as to the first issue in defendant¿s motion currently set for hearing on December 19 and denied as to all other issues in that motion. The first issue in the current motion s duplicative of the first issue in the summary motion heard by Judge Giorgi on September 8 and defendant has not presented any new facts, law or circumstances establishing good cause for the court to revisit the issue under either CCP 437c(f)(2) or 1008. All other issues in the second motion were not previously addressed and are therefore proper under Nieto v. Blue Shield of California Life & Health Ins. Co. (2010) 181 Cal.App.4th 60, 72. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. = (302/HEK)

rulings.Case Title: ROBERT MEYER et al VS. DEAN ELCHINOFF et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion To Compel Further Respoens To Request For Production Documents, Set Three, And Request For Sanctions
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Tuesday, November 15, 2011, Line 2. PLAINTIFF ROBERT MEYER AND DIANNE BOATE'S Notice Of Motion To Compel Further Respoens To Request For Production Documents, Set Three, And Request For Sanctions. Granted. Cross-complainant must pay sanctions to cross-defendants of $840 ($400 per hour for two hours plus $40 in costs) no later than December 15, 2011. Where, as here, a party seeks attorney¿s fees as damages, not as costs, the opposing party is entitled to full discovery of the amount and bases of the fees claimed, notwithstanding that fees information is normally immune from discovery as privileged. Cross-complainant¿s position lacks substantial justification. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. = (302/HEK)

rulings.Case Title: ROBERT MEYER et al VS. DEAN ELCHINOFF et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion To Compel Further Responses To Special Interrogatories, Set Four And Request For Sanctions
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Tuesday, November 15, 2011, Line 2. PLAINTIFFs ROBERT MEYER and DIANNE BOATE'S Notice Of Motion To Compel Further Responses To Special Interrogatories, Set Four And Request For Sanctions. Granted. Cross-complainant must pay sanctions to cross-defendants of $1240 ($400 per hour for three hours plus $40 in costs) no later than December 15, 2011. Where, as here, a party seeks attorney¿s fees as damages, not as costs, the opposing party is entitled to full discovery of the amount and bases of the fees claimed, notwithstanding that fees information is normally immune from discovery as privileged. Cross-complainant¿s position lacks substantial justification. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. rulings.Case Title: SALVATORE MINEO INDIVIDUALLY, AND ON BEHALF OF ALL et al VS. CITY SIGHTSEEING CORPORATION, A DELAWARE et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION To Quash Service Of Summons
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Tuesday, November 15, 2011, Line 6. DEFENDANT CITY SIGHTSEEING LTD.'S MOTION To Quash Service Of Summons. Granted. Service on the moving defendant was defective, but, even if that service was not defective, the moving defendant would still be entitled to quash service on it due to plaintiffs¿ failure to present facts showing that requiring the moving defendant to defend this action in California comports with due process. Plaintiffs have not shown that the moving defendant is subject to either general or specific jurisdiction. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. = (302/HEK)

rulings.Case Title: CARMEN ALUY et al VS. BANK OF AMERICA N.A et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Tuesday, November 15, 2011, Line 10, DEFENDANT RICK MOUSAW DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT. Overruled as to all four causes of action. Plaintiff sufficiently pleads an actual controversy between the parties. Defendant¿s alleged use of the line of credit that was secured by plaintiffs¿ property is a sufficient claim or interest in the property to support a quiet title claim. Plaintiffs may pursue their alternative claims for indemnity and unjust enrichment now in the event that the trier of fact determines that the line of credit is a debt enforceable against plaintiffs¿ property. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. = (302/HEK)

rulings.Case Title: SHIRLEY L. MARUSHIA VS. JOHNSON & JOHNSON SERVICES, INC. et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION TO ADMIT COUNSEL PRO HAC VICE
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Tuesday, November 15, 2011, Line 14, PLAINTIFF SHIRLEY MARUSHIA's MOTION TO ADMIT COUNSEL PRO HAC VICE. Hearing required for the court to determine whether Mr. Jez¿s applications to be admitted in ten different cases runs afoul of the rule that repeated pro hac vice appearances are usually prohibited. (Weil & Brown 9:402). The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. = (302/HEK)
